title: Is there any video file type that previews in browser - without having to download first?
excerpt: We upload a lot of videos. They are uploaded in MP4 standard, from a Monday Form. However, when we want to view the videos, we have to download them… :expressionless: :no_mouth: Is there any way, or any file type that ca…

posts.author: andyroo182 (Andrew Humphries)
posts.cookedHtml:
We upload a lot of videos. They are uploaded in MP4 standard, from a Monday Form. However, when we want to view the videos, we have to download them… Is there any way, or any file type that can be previewed in the browser?

Thanks!

MP4 Video Playback1915×1010 102 KB

posts.author: nealb (Neal Bailey)
posts.cookedHtml: MP4 I would use handbrake to convert

posts.author: andyroo182 (Andrew Humphries)
posts.cookedHtml: Thanks @nealb do you know what video types can be played directly in the Files column?

posts.author: nealb (Neal Bailey)
posts.cookedHtml: none I believe which is kind of a shame

posts.author: Alex.Robinson (Alex Robinson)
posts.cookedHtml:
@andyroo182 be happy to let you know that we currently have an App in development which will allow you to do exactly this.
If you would like to have a chat about any specific requirements you may have please feel free to book via below link and we can help you out.
Book Meeting

Thank you from the Team here at JT’s Cloud
